title: Basic Move
description: When you assign a heap-allocated value to another variable, ownership moves. The original variable becomes invalid.

steps.label: Create String
steps.annotation: s1 owns the String. It stores a pointer, length, and capacity on the stack, pointing to heap data.

steps.label: Move to s2
steps.annotation: Ownership moves from s1 to s2. s1 is now invalid — using it would cause error E0382.

steps.label: s2 goes out of scope
steps.annotation: When s2 goes out of scope, drop() is called, freeing the heap memory. Only one drop — no double-free!
